steps.title: Calculate and compare the means
steps.content:
Sum from the frequency table: $$13(12) + 14(8) + 15(5) + 16(7) + 17(9) + 18(10) + 19(13) + 20(7) = 1175$$

Original mean: $\frac{1175}{71}$

New mean (after adding 39): $\frac{1175+39}{72} = \frac{1214}{72}$

To compare, cross-multiply: $1175 \times 72 = 84,600$ and $1214 \times 71 = 86,194$. Since \$84,600 < 86,194$, we have $\frac{1175}{71} < \frac{1214}{72}$. The new mean is greater.

steps.title: Calculate and compare the medians
steps.content:
Original median (71 values): The median is at position $\frac{71+1}{2} = 36$. Using cumulative frequencies—12, 20, 25, 32, 41—the 36th value falls in the weight-17 group. Original median = 17.

New median (72 values): The median is the average of positions 36 and 37. Since 39 is the largest weight in the dataset, it occupies position 72 and does not affect positions 36–37. Both positions 36 and 37 contain weight 17. New median = $\frac{17+17}{2} = 17$.

The medians are equal.

steps.title: Match to the answer choice
steps.content: The new mean is greater than the original mean, and the medians of the two data sets are equal. That matches choice B — B is correct.
